summary: Matched Ness function func_80114968 by replacing an inline PowerPC asm block with C and updating the header prototype to include the second s32 argument. The function checks Ness's held item state via func_8026B2B4 and func_8026B320, maps item-state values 1 through 4 to func_80070FB4 calls with mode values 1, 0, 2, and 3, and conditionally calls func_80070C48 when arg1 is nonzero. The PR was described by the author as a simple match based on similar matches for other characters.
what_changed: Removed the inline asm implementation of func_80114968 from src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.c and replaced it with a C implementation.
Changed func_80114968 from taking only HSD_GObj* gobj to taking HSD_GObj* gobj and s32 arg1.
Updated the declaration in src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.h to match the newly recovered two-argument signature.
Recovered the use of gobj->user_data as Fighter* and ft->x1974_heldItem as the item passed to func_8026B2B4 and func_8026B320.
Represented the asm compare/branch chain over func_8026B320's return value as a switch with cases 1, 2, 3, and 4.
Preserved the early-out behavior: func_80070FB4 and the optional func_80070C48 call only happen when func_8026B2B4 returns 0.
smart_moves: Recognized that register r4 was a real second parameter, not a local value, and fixed the function prototype accordingly.
Converted a dense asm branch ladder into a clear switch over the held-item state result, preserving the exact case-to-argument mapping used by func_80070FB4.
Kept the conditional arg1 check after the switch and inside the func_8026B2B4 == 0 guard, matching the original control flow.
Used existing typed fighter fields, especially gobj->user_data and x1974_heldItem, rather than pointer arithmetic in the C version.
Leveraged similarity to other character functions, according to the PR body, to quickly resolve a small fighter-specific match.
decomp_lessons: When an asm function stores incoming r4 into a callee-saved register and later tests it, that value may indicate a missing formal parameter in the current prototype.
Item-state dispatch patterns can often be expressed as switch statements if the branch ladder compares against small integer return values.
Header declarations should be updated immediately when matching reveals missing parameters; otherwise call sites and future matches may preserve an incorrect signature.
A successful match can come from reusing patterns seen in similar character code, but the PR evidence only states this generally and does not identify the other characters.
Keep guard structure faithful: in this function both the state switch and optional follow-up call are skipped when func_8026B2B4 reports nonzero.
naming_conventions: Unidentified matched functions retained their generated names, such as func_80114968, func_8026B2B4, func_8026B320, func_80070FB4, and func_80070C48.
The recovered local variable switched_res describes the value used by the switch, though it is not a final semantic name.
The new second parameter was named arg1, reflecting that its semantic meaning was not yet recovered.
The fighter pointer follows the common local name ft after assigning Fighter* ft = gobj->user_data.
The held item field is accessed through the existing struct field name x1974_heldItem.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced explicit labels lbl_801149C0, lbl_801149CC, lbl_801149E0, lbl_801149F4, lbl_80114A08, lbl_80114A18, and lbl_80114A2C with structured C control flow.

Matched the asm's case behavior: held-item state 1 calls func_80070FB4(gobj, 1, 1), state 2 calls func_80070FB4(gobj, 1, 0), state 3 calls func_80070FB4(gobj, 1, 2), and state 4 calls func_80070FB4(gobj, 1, 3).
Recovered the final conditional call from the r30/r4 test as if (arg1 != 0) func_80070C48(gobj, 1).
The C replacement removed the surrounding pragma pop/push and peephole directives that were only needed around the previous asm block.
The declaration change in ftness.h was part of the matching tactic because the old one-argument prototype could not represent the observed r4 input.
follow_up_queries: Find other character functions similar to Ness func_80114968 that dispatch on func_8026B320 held-item states.
Search call sites of func_80114968 to determine the semantic meaning of arg1.
Investigate func_8026B2B4 and func_8026B320 to name the held-item status checks more precisely.
Compare func_80070FB4 argument mappings across fighter item-handling functions.
